hypothesis: If crystal material property prediction integrates complementary information from graph structural, natural language text, and X-ray diffraction spectral modalities through a cross-modal predictive learning architecture, then it will achieve lower mean absolute error than single-modality baselines (CGCNN, CoGN, ALIGNN) across multiple Matbench benchmark tasks, because each modality captures distinct physicochemical features that collectively provide a richer latent representation of material structure than any single modality alone.
